updates.summary: This is a significant minor version jump from 1.40.0 to 1.61.1 spanning 21 minor versions, which likely includes numerous feature additions, deprecations, and behavioral changes beyond what is shown in this partial changelog. The visible changelog focuses on bug fixes including regressions in ESM/sync loader behavior and Node 22.x compatibility issues. The large version gap warrants careful review as intermediate versions may contain breaking behavioral changes not captured here.
breakingChanges: Custom Expect.Extend matchers with the same name as default matchers could have been silently overriding default behavior — fix may alter existing test outcomes if relying on the previous (buggy) behavior
Sync ESM loader (registerHooks) behavior for extensionless .ts subpath imports in pnpm workspaces has changed — projects using pnpm with TypeScript path aliases may be affected
Node 22.15 compatibility fix for sync loader may introduce subtle loader differences for projects targeting Node 22
ramifications: 21 minor versions of accumulated changes may include deprecated APIs that have since been removed or altered
Test suite reliability could be affected by the Expect.Extend matcher fix if any custom matchers share names with built-in matchers
WebSocket trace viewer timing fix (1000x scale correction) could affect test debugging workflows relying on recorded traces
UI mode API request context byte reporting fix may change observed test output in CI pipelines using UI mode
Projects using pnpm with TypeScript and extensionless imports may need configuration adjustments after the ESM loader fix
Browser binaries may need to be re-downloaded as Playwright bundles specific browser versions per release

updates.summary: This minor update to ESLint 10.6.0 includes two feature additions to the no-constant-binary-expression rule and ten bug fixes addressing false positives and incorrect autofixes in various rules. There are no breaking changes, and the fixes improve accuracy and reliability of existing lint rules. The update is low-risk and beneficial for most projects.
ramifications: The new 'checkRelationalComparisons' option in no-constant-binary-expression may surface previously unreported lint errors if enabled, but it is opt-in so existing configs are unaffected.
Detection of Symbol() and BigInt() in no-constant-binary-expression may flag new issues in existing codebases that use these constructs in binary expressions.
Bug fixes to prefer-exponentiation-operator autofix may change previously incorrect automated code transformations, producing more accurate results.

Fixes to no-extra-boolean-cast, radix, no-throw-literal, prefer-promise-reject-errors, and max-nested-callbacks rules may reduce false positives, potentially changing the number of lint warnings or errors reported.
The fix to max-classes-per-file report range and max-nested-callbacks callback detection logic could alter the line/column reported for existing violations.

advisories.summary: Withdrawn Advisory: eslint has a Stack Overflow when serializing objects with circular references

updates.summary: This is a minor update to the 'globals' package that appears to add or update global variable definitions. The changelog is minimal, referencing only a globals update commit, suggesting low-risk additive changes such as new browser/runtime globals. This type of update is routine and unlikely to introduce breaking changes.
ramifications: New global variable definitions may be added, potentially affecting linting rules that rely on this package
ESLint or other tools consuming this package may now recognize additional globals without requiring custom configuration
Minimal risk of any existing behavior changing since this is a data-only package

updates.summary: This is a minor patch update (two patch versions) for html-validate with no changelog provided. Patch updates in well-maintained packages like html-validate typically include bug fixes and minor improvements without breaking changes. The risk is very low and upgrading is generally safe.
ramifications: No changelog was provided, so the exact changes are unknown, but patch versions rarely introduce breaking changes.
HTML validation rules or behavior may have minor bug fixes that could alter validation output in edge cases.
Skipping two patch versions (11.5.3 to 11.5.5) means two patch releases are included, slightly increasing the surface area of changes.

updates.summary: This minor update to Prettier includes a single formatting change for Angular's `@content(name)` syntax, adding a space before the parenthesis to align with other block syntax. The change is narrow in scope and only affects Angular template files using `@content` with a named slot. Teams not using Angular or this specific syntax will see no formatting differences.
breakingChanges: Angular templates using `@content(name)` will be reformatted to `@content (name)`, which may produce a large diff in Angular projects using this syntax
ramifications: Angular projects using `@content(name)` will have their formatting changed, potentially causing noisy git diffs on first run
CI pipelines with strict Prettier formatting checks may fail until files are reformatted
Non-Angular projects are completely unaffected by this change
No API or behavioral changes outside of formatting output

updates.summary: This patch update addresses a ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) vulnerability in the serve-handler dependency and bumps ajv to a newer version. Both changes are security-focused with no breaking API changes expected. Upgrading is strongly recommended to mitigate the ReDoS vulnerability.
ramifications: Fixes a ReDoS vulnerability in serve-handler 6.1.7, reducing denial-of-service attack surface for applications serving static files.
Bumping ajv to 8.18.0 may introduce minor behavioral differences in JSON schema validation, though this is unlikely to affect typical usage.
No API or behavioral breaking changes are expected from either dependency update.

advisories.summary: vercel/serve allows access to restricted files if filename is URL encoded.
Byass due to validation before canonicalization in serve
Information Exposure on Case Insensitive File Systems in serve

updates.summary: This is a low-risk minor update containing 3 bug fixes: two performance improvements and one false negative fix for the `function-calc-no-unspaced-operator` rule. The false negative fix means stylelint will now correctly flag previously missed violations involving unspaced `+` and `-` operators after `*` or `/` operators. No breaking changes are introduced.
ramifications: The fix for `function-calc-no-unspaced-operator` false negatives may cause new linting errors to surface in codebases that have unspaced `+` or `-` operators following `*` or `/` operators in calc() expressions, requiring CSS fixes.
Performance improvements to module path resolution and dynamic TIMING import may slightly reduce linting startup and execution time.
CI/CD pipelines that previously passed lint checks may now fail if affected calc() expressions exist in the codebase.

advisories.summary: Stylelint has vulnerability in semver dependency
